headline: Anthropic reversed a policy that would have quietly degraded Claude for frontier LLM work while pricing complaints over its Fable model mounted.
body: Anthropic walked back a policy that could have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier model development, acknowledging a wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards, drawing public criticism including from Redis creator Antirez [4][10]. The launch of Claude Fable 5 brought capability demos across vision, ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, and medical imaging alongside complaints that the model burns quota far faster than Opus, and OpenAI is reportedly weighing price cuts to compete [5][6][8]. On the model side, NVIDIA shipped its Nemotron 3 series combining hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with a Latent Mixture of Experts and multi-token prediction, aligning with transformer architecture and multi-token prediction breakouts [0]. AI-assisted development continues to lead and break out, while public skepticism toward AI sharpened across new data [7].

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
